title: Accessibility Engineer
description: Ensures digital products are usable by people with disabilities, following accessibility standards and inclusive design principles.

title: Accessibility UX Specialist
description: Ensures products are accessible to people with disabilities. Advocates inclusive design in digital products and physical spaces.

title: Acoustical Engineer
description: Acoustical Engineers manage sound and vibration in buildings, vehicles, and products. They improve sound quality while reducing noise pollution.

title: Aerospace Engineer
description: Aerospace Engineers design, test, and improve aircraft, rockets, satellites, and spacecraft. They work on aerodynamics, propulsion, materials, and structural systems to ensure safety and performance in extreme conditions.

title: AI Ethics Specialist
description: Evaluates and guides the responsible development of AI systems, focusing on fairness, bias, transparency, and societal impact. Works closely with engineers, legal teams, and policymakers.

title: AI Research Scientist
description: AI Research Scientists develop new artificial intelligence methods and algorithms. Their work pushes the boundaries of what machines can do.

title: Air Quality Analyst
description: Air Quality Analysts monitor and model pollutants to assess environmental health risks. Their work informs regulations and public health recommendations.

title: Astrophysicist
description: Astrophysicists study stars, galaxies, and cosmic phenomena to understand the universe. Research can involve telescopes, simulations, and theoretical models.

title: Augmented Reality Developer
description: AR Developers create experiences that blend digital content with the real world. Their work is used in gaming, education, and training.

title: Automotive Design Engineer
description: Automotive Design Engineers develop vehicle components such as chassis, engines, safety systems, and drivetrains. They balance performance, efficiency, cost, and manufacturability while working closely with manufacturing teams.

title: Autonomous Vehicle Engineer
description: Autonomous Vehicle Engineers design self-driving systems, integrating sensors, AI, and vehicle controls. Their work advances transportation safety and automation.

title: Back-End Developer
description: Back-End Developers build server-side systems, databases, and APIs that power applications. They focus on performance, security, and scalability.

title: Backend Software Engineer
description: Backend Software Engineers build server-side systems that handle data processing, authentication, and scalability. They ensure reliability and performance behind the scenes.

title: Biomechanical Engineer
description: Biomechanical Engineers apply mechanical principles to biological systems such as joints, muscles, and medical implants. Their work improves mobility and health outcomes.

title: Biostatistician
description: Analyzes biological and medical data to support clinical research and public health decisions. Ensures studies are statistically valid and reliable.

title: Blockchain Developer
description: Blockchain Developers build decentralized applications and secure digital ledgers. Their work supports cryptocurrencies and distributed systems.

title: Chemical Process Engineer
description: Chemical Process Engineers design and optimize industrial processes that convert raw materials into usable products like fuels, plastics, and pharmaceuticals. Their work focuses on efficiency, safety, and large-scale production reliability.

title: Civil Structural Engineer
description: Civil Structural Engineers design buildings, bridges, and infrastructure to withstand loads, weather, and long-term use. They ensure structures meet safety codes while remaining cost-effective and durable.

title: Climate Change Analyst
description: Climate Change Analysts study trends, risks, and impacts of global climate change. They model scenarios and propose mitigation strategies.

title: Clinical Research Coordinator
description: Clinical Research Coordinators organize and oversee medical trials, ensuring protocols are followed and data is accurately collected. They support the development of new therapies and medical devices.

title: Cloud Architect
description: Cloud Architects design large-scale cloud infrastructure for applications used worldwide. They balance cost, performance, and reliability.

title: Computational Biologist
description: Uses algorithms and simulations to study biological systems such as protein folding or gene regulation. Plays a major role in modern biomedical research.

title: Computer Vision Engineer
description: Computer Vision Engineers develop systems that interpret images and video. Applications include self-driving cars and medical imaging.

title: Conservation Scientist
description: Develops strategies to protect ecosystems, wildlife, and natural resources using data and field research. Often works with governments and nonprofits.
employers: The Nature Conservancy, WWF, US Fish & Wildlife Service

title: Construction Engineer / Manager
description: Construction Engineers manage the execution of engineering designs on job sites. They coordinate schedules, materials, safety, and teams to ensure projects are completed efficiently and correctly.

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
description: Cybersecurity Analysts protect systems from digital attacks. They monitor threats, investigate vulnerabilities, and strengthen defenses.

title: Data Scientist
description: Data Scientists analyze large datasets to uncover patterns and insights. Their findings influence business, science, and policy decisions.

title: Database Administrator
description: Database Administrators manage and optimize databases that store critical information. They ensure data integrity, security, and performance.

title: Design Strategist
description: Design Strategists use design thinking to align product design with business strategy and user needs. Guides long-term vision and innovation.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: DevOps Engineers automate software deployment and infrastructure management. They ensure systems are reliable, scalable, and efficient.

title: Digital Health Product Specialist
description: Works on software platforms that support telemedicine, patient monitoring, and health analytics. Bridges healthcare needs with technology solutions.

title: Ecological Restoration Specialist
description: Plans and manages projects that restore damaged ecosystems such as wetlands and forests. Combines science, engineering, and land management.

title: Electrical Power Systems Engineer
description: These engineers design and maintain systems that generate, transmit, and distribute electrical power. Their work increasingly involves renewable energy and smart grid technologies.
employers: Siemens, GE, National Grid, Duke Energy

title: Embedded Systems Developer
description: Embedded Systems Developers write software that runs directly on hardware devices. These systems control medical devices, vehicles, and electronics.

title: Energy Systems Engineer
description: Energy Systems Engineers design systems that generate, store, and distribute energy efficiently. They often work on smart grids and alternative energy solutions.
employers: Duke Energy, Siemens Energy, GE Vernova

title: Environmental Data Modeler
description: Uses computational models to simulate environmental systems such as climate, pollution spread, or land use. Supports policy and scientific decision-making.

title: Environmental Engineer
description: Environmental Engineers develop solutions to problems like water pollution, waste management, and environmental contamination. Their work protects ecosystems while supporting sustainable development.

title: Environmental Health & Safety Specialist
description: EHS Specialists ensure workplaces comply with safety and environmental regulations. They evaluate hazards, train staff, and implement preventative measures.

title: Environmental Planner
description: Environmental Planners integrate ecological considerations into urban and regional development. They evaluate projects for sustainability, safety, and compliance.

title: Front-End Web Developer
description: Front-End Developers build interactive and visually appealing user interfaces. They ensure websites are responsive, accessible, and engaging.

title: Frontend Engineer
description: Frontend Engineers develop the visual and interactive components of websites and applications. They focus on performance, accessibility, and user experience.

title: Full-Stack Engineer
description: Full-Stack Engineers work on both the front-end and back-end of applications. They build complete systems from user interface to database.

title: Game Designer
description: Game Designers define gameplay mechanics, storylines, and user engagement for video games. Collaborates with developers and artists.

title: Game Developer
description: Game Developers design and program video games, working on gameplay, graphics, and AI. They collaborate with artists and designers.

title: Geotechnical Engineer
description: Geotechnical Engineers analyze soil, rock, and underground conditions to ensure foundations and infrastructure are safe and stable. Their work is critical for construction projects of all sizes.

title: Hardware Design Engineer
description: Hardware Design Engineers develop electronic components and computer hardware. Their work supports modern computing systems.

title: Healthcare Data Analyst
description: Healthcare Data Analysts analyze medical data to improve patient outcomes, efficiency, and operational decisions. They work with electronic health records, predictive models, and analytics platforms.

title: Human Factors Engineer
description: Improves usability and safety of systems by applying knowledge of human behavior, capabilities, and limitations. Often works in medical, aviation, or industrial fields.

title: Human-Centered Design Researcher
description: Conducts studies on human needs and behaviors to inform design decisions. Integrates ethnography, interviews, and data analysis.

title: Human-Robot Interaction Engineer
description: Designs systems that allow humans and robots to work together safely and intuitively, often focusing on behavior, interfaces, and trust. This role blends engineering with psychology and usability testing.
employers: Boston Dynamics, Toyota Research Institute, NASA JPL

title: Human-Robot Interaction Researcher
description: These researchers study how humans interact with robots to make systems intuitive, safe, and effective. Work involves psychology, design, and engineering.
employers: MIT CSAIL, Toyota Research Institute, Boston Dynamics

title: Hydrologist
description: Hydrologists study the movement, distribution, and quality of water on Earth. Their work informs water resource management, flood prevention, and environmental protection.

title: Immunologist
description: Immunologists study the immune system to understand diseases, develop vaccines, and design therapies. Their research can impact both human and animal health.

title: Industrial Designer
description: Creates consumer products considering usability, aesthetics, and manufacturability. Work ranges from electronics to furniture.

title: Industrial Engineer
description: Industrial Engineers improve efficiency in systems such as factories, hospitals, and supply chains. They analyze workflows, data, and processes to reduce waste and improve productivity.

title: Information Architect
description: Information Architects structure digital content and navigation to make information accessible and logical for users.

title: Intelligent Systems Engineer
description: Builds systems that combine sensing, reasoning, and action, often used in smart factories or autonomous platforms. Focuses on system-level intelligence rather than isolated algorithms.

title: Interaction Designer
description: Interaction Designers focus on how users engage with digital systems through workflows, animations, and feedback. Goal is intuitive and satisfying user experiences.

title: Interactive Media Designer
description: Develops digital experiences such as apps, installations, or games that respond to user input. Combines design, coding, and storytelling.

title: IT Support Specialist
description: IT Support Specialists help users resolve technical issues and maintain systems. They ensure technology works smoothly for organizations.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description: Machine Learning Engineers develop AI systems that learn from data. They deploy models into real products such as recommendations or predictions.

title: Manufacturing Process Engineer
description: Manufacturing Process Engineers improve production processes to increase efficiency, quality, and safety in factories. They work closely with automation and quality teams.

title: Marine Engineer
description: Marine Engineers design and maintain ships, submarines, and offshore platforms. Their work ensures propulsion, power, and mechanical systems operate reliably in harsh ocean environments.
employers: Rolls-Royce Marine, Naval Sea Systems Command, Wärtsilä

title: Materials Engineer
description: Materials Engineers develop and test materials used in electronics, aerospace, medical devices, and consumer products. They focus on improving strength, durability, conductivity, or heat resistance.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Mechanical Design Engineers create mechanical components and systems using CAD software, simulations, and prototypes. They translate ideas into manufacturable products.
employers: Caterpillar, Dyson, Parker Hannifin, John Deere

title: Mechatronics Engineer
description: Mechatronics Engineers integrate mechanical systems, electronics, sensors, and software into unified products such as robots and smart machines.

title: Medical Imaging Scientist
description: Develops imaging techniques such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound to improve diagnostics. Works at the intersection of physics, software, and medicine.

title: Mining / Resource Extraction Engineer
description: These engineers plan and manage the extraction of minerals and natural resources. They focus on efficiency, worker safety, and environmental responsibility.

title: Mobile App Developer
description: Mobile App Developers create applications for smartphones and tablets. They focus on performance, usability, and user engagement.

title: Natural Language Processing Engineer
description: Creates systems that allow computers to understand, generate, and interact using human language. Applications include chatbots, translation tools, and voice assistants.

title: Network Engineer
description: Network Engineers design and maintain communication networks that connect systems and users. Reliability and performance are critical to their work.

title: Neuroengineer
description: Neuroengineers design technology that interacts with the brain and nervous system, including brain-computer interfaces and neural prosthetics. Their work combines biology, engineering, and software to restore or enhance neurological functions.
employers: Neuralink, Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Lab, Medtronic

title: Nuclear Engineer
description: Nuclear Engineers work with nuclear systems for energy production, medical applications, or research. Safety, regulation, and precision are central to their role.
employers: Westinghouse, Department of Energy, GE Hitachi

title: Optical Engineer
description: Optical Engineers design lenses, sensors, lasers, and imaging systems. Work spans cameras, telescopes, medical devices, and lasers.

title: Particle Physicist
description: Researches fundamental particles and forces using accelerators and detectors. This role often contributes to foundational discoveries in physics.

title: Petroleum Engineer
description: Petroleum Engineers design methods to extract oil and gas efficiently from underground reservoirs. They use modeling and drilling technologies to maximize output and safety.

title: Planetary Scientist
description: Planetary Scientists study planets, moons, and planetary systems. Work includes space missions, lab simulations, and data analysis from spacecraft.

title: Plasma Physicist
description: Studies ionized gases used in fusion research, space propulsion, and astrophysical phenomena. Research often supports next-generation energy or space systems.

title: Product Designer
description: Product Designers design physical or digital products from concept to completion. They consider usability, aesthetics, and manufacturing or technical feasibility.

title: Product Manager (Tech)
description: Product Managers define product goals and coordinate teams to deliver technology solutions. They balance user needs and technical constraints.

title: QA Test Engineer (Software)
description: QA Test Engineers test software to find bugs and usability issues. Their work ensures reliable and polished products.

title: Quality Assurance Engineer
description: Quality Assurance Engineers test products and systems to ensure they meet performance and safety standards. They identify issues before products reach users.

title: Quantum Computing Researcher
description: Develops quantum algorithms, hardware, and software for next-generation computing. Combines physics, math, and computer science.

title: Quantum Systems Engineer
description: Develops hardware and control systems for quantum computers and sensors. Combines physics, engineering, and precision instrumentation.

title: Reinforcement Learning Engineer
description: Develops AI systems that learn through trial and error, commonly used in robotics, games, and autonomous systems. This work often involves simulations and large-scale experimentation.

title: Reliability Engineer
description: Reliability Engineers analyze failures and design improvements to increase product lifespan and system uptime. Their work prevents costly breakdowns.

title: Renewable Energy Systems Engineer
description: Designs and evaluates solar, wind, and energy storage systems to improve efficiency and reliability. Works on both grid-scale and localized energy solutions.

title: RF (Radio Frequency) Engineer
description: RF Engineers design wireless communication systems for satellites, radar, and telecom. Their work ensures reliable signal transmission and reception.

title: Robotics Systems Engineer
description: Robotics Systems Engineers integrate hardware, sensors, and software into complete robotic systems. They ensure robots perform tasks accurately and safely.

title: Robotics Technician
description: Robotics Technicians install, maintain, and repair robotic systems in manufacturing, research, and service applications. Their work ensures operational reliability.

title: Safety / Health Engineer
description: Safety Engineers design systems and procedures to prevent workplace injuries and accidents. They analyze risks and ensure compliance with safety regulations.

title: Sanitation / Water Treatment Engineer
description: These engineers design systems that deliver clean drinking water and treat wastewater safely. Their work directly supports public health and environmental protection.

title: Satellite Operations Engineer
description: Monitors and controls satellites after launch, ensuring communication, navigation, and data collection systems function correctly. Often works with ground stations and mission control teams.

title: Service Designer
description: Service Designers plan end-to-end user experiences across touchpoints, integrating processes, tools, and people.

title: Site Reliability Engineer
description: Site Reliability Engineers ensure large-scale systems stay online and performant. They combine software engineering with operations.
good_fit_for: Reliability-focused, analytical, calm under pressure, systematic

title: Software Developer
description: Software Developers build applications and systems used on computers, phones, and the web. They write, test, and maintain code to solve real-world problems.

title: Software Test Automation Engineer
description: Software Test Automation Engineers design automated testing frameworks to ensure software reliability and prevent bugs. They play a key role in quality assurance.

title: Soil Scientist
description: Soil Scientists study soil composition, health, and suitability for agriculture or construction. Their research supports farming, conservation, and land management.
employers: USDA, consulting firms, agricultural research institutes

title: Space Mission Planner
description: Designs mission timelines, trajectories, and operational strategies for space missions. Balances fuel efficiency, scientific goals, and safety constraints.

title: Space Systems Engineer
description: Space Systems Engineers design spacecraft and mission systems, integrating propulsion, electronics, and communications. They ensure missions are technically feasible and safe.

title: Space Weather Analyst
description: Monitors solar activity and space conditions that can affect satellites, power grids, and communications. Provides warnings and mitigation strategies.

title: Speech Recognition Engineer
description: Speech Recognition Engineers develop AI systems that understand and process human speech. Applications include voice assistants and transcription software.

title: Sustainability Consultant
description: Sustainability Consultants advise organizations on reducing environmental impact, improving resource efficiency, and implementing green initiatives.

title: Systems / Controls Engineer
description: Systems and Controls Engineers design automated control systems for machines, robots, and industrial processes. They ensure systems respond accurately and efficiently.

title: Technical Writer (Software)
description: Technical Writers create clear documentation for complex software systems. They translate technical concepts into understandable language.

title: Toxicologist
description: Toxicologists investigate how chemicals, drugs, and environmental substances affect living systems. Their findings help ensure safety in medicine, consumer products, and industry.

title: Transportation / Traffic Engineer
description: Transportation Engineers design and improve roadways, intersections, and transit systems. Their work increases safety and reduces congestion.

title: UI Designer
description: UI Designers focus on the visual design of applications, including colors, typography, and layout. Ensures interfaces are clear and appealing.

title: UX Designer
description: UX Designers create products that are intuitive, efficient, and enjoyable for users. They conduct research, prototype, and iterate designs.

title: UX Engineer
description: UX Engineers combine front-end development with user experience design. They ensure digital products are intuitive and accessible.

title: UX Researcher
description: Conducts user studies, interviews, and usability tests to inform product design decisions. Ensures products meet real user needs.

title: Visual Designer
description: Visual Designers create graphics, illustrations, and visual branding. They communicate messages clearly and aesthetically.

title: Voice UX Designer
description: Designs voice interfaces for assistants, smart devices, and apps. Focuses on conversational flow and natural interactions.

title: VR/AR Experience Designer
description: Designs immersive virtual and augmented reality experiences for entertainment, training, or education. Combines interaction, graphics, and storytelling.

title: Wildlife Biologist
description: Wildlife Biologists research animal populations, behavior, and habitats. They support conservation efforts, policy, and ecological balance.
employers: National Park Service, WWF, state wildlife agencies

title: Wind Energy Engineer
description: Wind Energy Engineers design and optimize wind turbines and wind farms. Their work advances renewable energy adoption and efficiency.
employers: Vestas, GE Renewable Energy, Siemens Gamesa
